pattern: Once every value collapses to just two possible labels, the final sorted layout is forced -- you never need to actually sort, just count which label wins how many times and lay zeros before ones.
hints: The final array only ever contains 0s and 1s, so its sorted shape is completely determined by how many of each you have.
Walk the input once and tally how many values are even and how many are odd.
Build the answer directly: the tally of evens gives you that many leading 0s, followed by the tally of odds as trailing 1s -- no comparison-based sort needed.

edge_inputs.description: mixed parity with a repeated value
already sorted by value but not by parity label, must still be relabeled and re-sorted

stress.description: random-length array of random values across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
